title: podman [COMMAND] panic: invalid page type: 23: 10
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug
（maybe）

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. I reboot my RHEL 8.7 OS;

2. I found my software cannot use after OS reboot , then I found podman has problem when I use "podman ps" command to check my container. It cannot output my all containers.

**Describe the results you received:**
"podman ps" output error, cannot output my container.

**Describe the results you expected:**
"podman ps" can output my container

**Additional [information](url) you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I use podman command output error:
```bash
~]# podman
Manage pods, containers and images

Usage:
  podman [options] [command]
...skipping...

Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND'
```

**Output of `podman version`:**
**Output of `podman info`:**

Cannot output of `podman version` or other COMMAND now. It will output same error messages.
like this: 
```
panic: invalid page type: 23: 10
```

Yes!
I installed the latest version of Podman from RHEL Repository now. And i tested reinstall old version but Podman cannot work current still.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AMD x86 physical (my pc)

comments.body:
I have solved this problem now. follow this solution from redhat: [podman commands crash with "panic: invalid page type: xxxxxxxxxxxxx: yyyy"](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/5191521) 

Remove boltdb file and files underoverlay-containers directory
```bash
# rm /var/lib/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db
# rm -rf /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/
```

And then I can use `podman ps`. But cannot list my containers because i deleted the `bolt_state.db` and `overlay-containers/`.
I have to rebuild my containers and get right again.

Example：
```bash
[root@my-pc qbittorrent]# ./start.sh 
8cd4f124c56d7125c40624ac889518d92099c96fed8163da7b72892ebfb257bd
[root@my-pc qbittorrent]# podman ps 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                     COMMAND               CREATED        STATUS                      PORTS                                                                   NAMES
8cd4f124c56d  docker.io/linuxserver/qbittorrent:latest                        7 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago            0.0.0.0:6881->6881/udp, 0.0.0.0:6881->6881/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8080->8080/tcp  qbittorrent
```
Glad you could resolve the issue, @shine-fire! Thanks for sharing the fix.
Another DB corruption issue with Bolt. Fourth since summer. Beginning to become seriously concerned.
